symbolic_system.boundary: Rules for citing sister repos as inputs without absorbing their canon, warrant, or action authority.

carrier.carrier_state: seed interface map with allowed imports, blocked imports, runtime uses, and non-laundering rules
carrier.carrier_state_space: repo roles, allowed imports, blocked imports, runtime-use clauses, non-laundering clauses
carrier.maintainer: RAE architecture docs and future governance updates

use_episode.use: Use interface cards to decide how RAE may cite sister-repo artifacts in situation packets and runtime work.
use_episode.inference_drawn: A sister repo may be cited only under the declared interface role and blocked-import constraints.
non_inferences: Citing a sister repo imports its domain warrant.
RAE may override a sister repo's canon by naming an interface role.

binding.binding_target: cross-repo reference boundaries and non-laundering conditions
binding.operative_mode: cross-repo citation and blocked-import rule surface

validity.validity_criterion: The interface card preserves repo role boundaries and blocks authority transfer outside declared scope.
validity.validity_evidence: The artifact explicitly states 'Reference is not absorption' and lists allowed and blocked imports for each sister repo.
validity.confidence: high for local interface intent; untested for repeated future integration work
validity.uncertainty: Standing is ambiguous when two repos disagree about whether a citation stays inside the declared interface role.

scope.scope_conditions: RAE family cross-repo references in situation packets, stack docs, workstream maps, and runtime orientation artifacts.
scope_lost: sister repo internal review state may change after citation
prohibited_scope_extensions: claim that interface role transfers canon authority

recognition_authority.recognizer_standing: RAE can define how it cites other repos, but sister repos retain standing over their own canon and warrant state.
recognition_authority.authority_source: RAE architecture docs for interface use; sister repos for their own canon and admitted claims.
recognition_authority.uptake_path: interface card -> situation packet citation -> sibling repo artifact reference -> bounded consequence

distributed_standing.standing_scope: define RAE-side citation roles and blocked imports
determine canonical status and warrant of sister-repo artifacts
cite sister artifacts only within declared interface role

policy_license.attempted_consequence: Use interface cards to route sister-repo references in RAE work.
policy_license.action_policy: Reference is not absorption; interface is not authority transfer.
policy_license.licensed_consequence: Bounded citation and role declaration for cross-repo references.
policy_license.license_basis: Declared allowed imports, blocked imports, non-laundering rules, and distributed standing.

transmission.transmission_path: interface card -> RAE packet or architecture doc -> future work decision
metadata_lost: future changes in source repo status unless rechecked

unlicensed_links: source repo canonical status must be checked at use time

verdict.next_check: Test whether future packet citations preserve source-repo status metadata.

review.promotion_authority: RAE maintainers for interface-card use only

reflexive_pressure.notes: Standing is genuinely distributed and partly ambiguous: RAE owns citation rules, not sister repo warrant.

symbolic_system.boundary: Boundary between standalone foundation derivations, hardening discipline, and apparatus or machine-facing application work.

carrier.carrier_state: separation note naming derivation track, hardening track, apparatus/application track, and non-scope
carrier.carrier_state_space: boundary rules, track classifications, promotion status claims, non-scope statements
carrier.maintainer: ACF foundation docs and repo maintenance workflow

use_episode.use: Use the boundary to decide whether a machine-facing artifact may be treated as foundation, application, or hardening pressure.
use_episode.inference_drawn: Machine tooling may refine, route, check, or pressure the foundation but does not make the foundation true.
non_inferences: A case-packet pressure result is itself a foundation theorem.

binding.binding_target: ACF track separation and promotion boundary
binding.operative_mode: boundary rule surface for foundation/application classification

validity.validity_criterion: The artifact prevents tool/application status from laundering into foundation truth or promotion status.
validity.validity_evidence: The document states that machinery can refine or pressure the foundation but does not make it true.
validity.uncertainty: The artifact does not by itself define who adjudicates contested promotion from hardening pressure into foundation doctrine.

scope.scope_conditions: ACF repo surfaces and bridge-facing application/tooling work.
scope_lost: future promotion decision procedure remains partly external

recognition_authority.recognizer_standing: ACF maintainers can classify repo surfaces; case-packet reviewers can create pressure; tooling workers can implement downstream applications without promoting foundation claims.
recognition_authority.authority_source: ACF boundary document and repo maintenance practice.
recognition_authority.uptake_path: boundary doc -> track classification -> promotion or quarantine decision

distributed_standing.standing_scope: classify foundation/application boundary and update doctrine
produce pressure, falsifier, or revision candidate
implement downstream application without truth promotion

policy_license.attempted_consequence: Use the boundary to classify an ACF-adjacent artifact's role.
policy_license.action_policy: Record derivation as derivation first; record machinery only as application or promotion pressure.
policy_license.licensed_consequence: Local classification of foundation, hardening, or application status.
policy_license.license_basis: Boundary rule, track definitions, and ACF maintenance standing.

transmission.transmission_path: ACF boundary doc -> cross-stack integration work -> classification or quarantine decision
metadata_lost: future promotion evidence unless separately attached

unlicensed_links: promotion from hardening pressure to foundation doctrine requires additional review

verdict.next_check: Define promotion standing if repeated cases need adjudication.

review.promotion_authority: ACF maintainers for ACF doctrine only

reflexive_pressure.notes: The scaffold forced an explicit gap: pressure-generation standing is not promotion standing.

symbolic_system.boundary: Report of exact duplicate and near-duplicate callable move names across SSA files.

carrier.carrier_state: collision report with catalog counts, exact duplicate move names, occurrence counts, and file references
carrier.carrier_state_space: move names, occurrence counts, file paths, duplicate groups, near-duplicate heuristic groups
carrier.maintainer: SSA report-generation tooling and reports directory

use_episode.use: Use the collision report to prioritize manual review of duplicate callable move names.
use_episode.actor_context: SSA library maintenance or FSST compiler source selection
use_episode.inference_drawn: A duplicate move name is a candidate collision requiring review, not automatically an error.
non_inferences: A duplicate name means the underlying structural operation is the same.
The report licenses deprecation, rename, or merge without maintainer review.

binding.binding_target: SSA callable move registry names and file occurrences
binding.operative_mode: triage surface for human registry maintenance

validity.validity_criterion: The report accurately detects duplicate names and preserves enough file context for review.
validity.validity_evidence: The report lists catalog entries, exact duplicate counts, near-duplicate groups, and file references.
validity.confidence: medium for syntactic duplicate detection; low for semantic collision classification
validity.uncertainty: Near-duplicate heuristics and semantic collision status are not validated by the report itself.

scope.scope_conditions: SSA callable move names present in the scanned catalog at report generation time.
scope_lost: current repo state after report generation

recognition_authority.recognizer_standing: The generator has standing to report syntactic collisions; SSA maintainers have standing to decide merge/deprecation; compiler users may only treat collisions as triage signals.
recognition_authority.authority_source: SSA report-generation process and maintainer review.
recognition_authority.uptake_path: generated report -> maintainer review -> optional registry correction

distributed_standing.standing_scope: detect duplicate move-name strings and report file references
decide whether duplicates are acceptable, mergeable, or defective
use report as a triage source only

policy_license.attempted_consequence: Use duplicate move findings to guide registry maintenance.
policy_license.action_policy: Generated collision reports are triage artifacts, not registry-change authority.
policy_license.licensed_consequence: Prioritize manual review of named duplicate move groups.
policy_license.license_basis: Syntactic duplicate evidence plus preserved file references.
blocked_consequences: claim that near-duplicates are actual collisions

transmission.transmission_path: SSA scan -> generated collision report -> maintainer or compiler triage
metadata_lost: semantic context of each move body

unlicensed_links: merge/deprecate authority is absent from generated report

verdict.next_check: Review a duplicate group manually before treating it as semantic collision.

review.promotion_authority: SSA maintainers for registry changes only

reflexive_pressure.notes: This was the most useful ambiguous case: the carrier is highly legible, but consequence authority is narrow.

source.artifact: Biological Maintenance To Cognitive Bounded Processing Boundary

symbolic_system.boundary: Candidate edge packet from biological maintenance constraints to cognitive bounded processing boundaries.

carrier.carrier_state: source-material draft with kernels, source regime, target candidate, edge fields, object terms, and claim ceiling
carrier.carrier_state_space: kernel references, edge id, source and target levels, mechanism families, object terms, weakest-link statements, non-admission status
carrier.maintainer: CDP graph derivation ladder and foundation discipline

use_episode.use: Use the packet as source material for derivation-ladder portability testing.
use_episode.actor_context: CDP derivation ladder review and FSST compiler batch trial
use_episode.inference_drawn: The packet may be treated as a bounded source-material candidate, not a production-admitted cognitive theory or edge.
non_inferences: The packet derives cognition from biology.
Cognitive-science terms have native warrant from this packet alone.
The packet licenses psychological or agency claims.

binding.binding_target: candidate relation between biological maintenance constraints and cognitive bounded processing boundaries

validity.validity_criterion: The packet preserves source kernels, source-regime status, weakest-link residue, and non-admission ceiling.
validity.validity_evidence: The document explicitly states source-material draft status, claim ceiling, source kernels, weakest link, and non-production-admitted status.
validity.confidence: medium for source-material routing; low for cognitive-native warrant
validity.uncertainty: Native cognitive/neuroscience warrant remains pending external source collection.

scope.scope_conditions: CDP derivation-ladder portability testing for a biological-maintenance to cognitive-processing boundary candidate.

recognition_authority.recognizer_standing: Ladder reviewers can treat the packet as source material; promoters require additional gate inputs; FSST compiler workers can compile the artifact but cannot promote CDP graph status.
recognition_authority.authority_source: CDP derivation ladder discipline and packet claim ceiling.
recognition_authority.uptake_path: source-material packet -> ladder review -> possible revision or future promotion gate

distributed_standing.standing_scope: review source-material candidate and weakest-link residue
only later production admission if gate requirements are satisfied
compile source packet into FSST compiler record for tooling pressure

policy_license.attempted_consequence: Use the packet as a batch source for compiler-record translation and derivation-ladder review pressure.
policy_license.action_policy: Source-material candidate status and CDP ladder discipline.
policy_license.licensed_consequence: Compiler-record translation and source-material review pressure only.
policy_license.license_basis: Explicit source-material status, claim ceiling, non-admission statement, and weakest-link residue.

transmission.transmission_path: CDP ladder packet -> FSST compiler batch record -> possible future CDP review
metadata_lost: full source-specific evidence for cognitive terms

unlicensed_links: production admission and native cognitive warrant are absent

verdict.next_check: Collect native cognitive/neuroscience warrant before any promotion claim.

review.promotion_authority: CDP promoter only after separate gate review

reflexive_pressure.notes: The scaffold forced preservation of non-admission status and native-warrant residue.
